name: First State Community Action Agency, Inc.
description_short: Delaware-based nonprofit community action agency providing emergency assistance, case management, housing counseling, and employment services to low-income families and individuals.
description_long:
First State Community Action Agency (FSCAA) is a Delaware-based nonprofit organization operating under the Community Action framework, with a mission to help low-income individuals and families move from crisis to self-sufficiency. The organization serves households at or below 200% of federal poverty guidelines and emphasizes a holistic, goal-directed approach to client support. FSCAA operates across multiple service areas including emergency assistance, housing support, workforce development, and community enrichment programs. The agency is grounded in the philosophy of "People Helping People Build Community" and focuses on helping clients help themselves through structured case management and skill-building opportunities.

FSCAA's service portfolio spans three major categories: client-based services (emergency aid, food pantry, case management), community development and housing (counseling, energy assistance, tax preparation), and employment and training programs (culinary arts, senior employment, youth workforce programs). Emergency services include rent and utility assistance, eviction prevention, medical expense support, and emergency shelter for domestic violence and fire victims. The organization also operates specialized programs including a Fatherhood Priority Program, State Rental Assistance Program (for individuals exiting state institutions and foster care), Second Chance Reentry (for incarcerated individuals 6-12 months pre-release), and Grant Diversion (one-time employment support up to $1,500 annually). Housing services include counseling, heater repair/replacement, and summer cooling assistance.

What distinguishes FSCAA is its integration of emergency relief with long-term workforce development and community building. Rather than solely providing crisis aid, the agency pairs immediate assistance with comprehensive case management, goal-setting, and barrier-mitigation strategies. The organization targets specific high-need populations including justice-involved individuals, aging-out foster youth, and non-custodial parents. FSCAA also uniquely combines wraparound services—food security, housing stability, employment training, and financial literacy—within a single agency structure rather than requiring clients to navigate multiple providers.

FSCAA is genuinely a free-help nonprofit and an appropriate fit for individuals seeking no-cost emergency assistance, case management, and workforce development. However, potential clients should note that most programs require registration and have eligibility requirements based on income guidelines. Some programs (SRAP, Grant Diversion) are referral-only, which may create access barriers. The organization is geographically limited to Delaware and does not offer credit repair, debt consolidation, or credit-building products—it focuses exclusively on poverty alleviation and community stability.
pros: Provides emergency rent and utility assistance to prevent eviction—critical for housing stability
Comprehensive case management with goal-setting and action planning, not just one-off aid
Specializes in hard-to-serve populations: justice-involved individuals, foster youth aging out, non-custodial parents
Integrated employment and workforce development programs (culinary arts, senior employment, youth jobs) paired with emergency services
Free EITC tax preparation and financial literacy training included in services
State Rental Assistance Program supports clients exiting institutions and psychiatric care
No-cost food pantry and emergency shelter for domestic violence/fire victims
cons: Geographic limitation: Delaware-only service area; not available in other states
Several programs (SRAP, Grant Diversion) are referral-only, limiting self-directed access
Income eligibility capped at 200% federal poverty guideline; above that threshold, clients ineligible
All services require registration (online or phone), which may create barriers for digitally disconnected populations
Grant Diversion assistance capped at $1,500 annually—insufficient for major crises
best_for: Low-income Delaware residents facing housing instability, eviction, or utility crises
Justice-involved individuals 6-12 months pre-release seeking employment and housing support
Non-custodial parents seeking job training and financial literacy to improve child support capacity
Youth aging out of foster care needing rental assistance and case management for independent living
diagnosis: First State Community Action Agency is best for Delaware residents earning at or below 200% of federal poverty guidelines who face immediate housing, food, or employment crises and need integrated emergency aid with case management. The primary caveat is geographic limitation (Delaware-only) and eligibility caps that exclude working poor above the poverty threshold; referral-only programs also restrict self-directed access.
services: Emergency Services (rent, utilities, eviction prevention, medical/prescription assistance, emergency shelter)
Food Pantry (3-day supply of food staples)
Comprehensive Case Management (goal-setting, action planning, barrier mitigation)
Fatherhood Priority Program (job training, adult education, financial literacy for non-custodial parents)
State Rental Assistance Program (housing support for foster youth, state facility exits, diversion clients)
Second Chance Reentry (case management for incarcerated individuals pre-release)
Grant Diversion (one-time employment support up to $1,500 for transportation, tools, uniforms, licenses)
Heater Repair/Replacement and Summer Cooling Assistance
Earned Income Tax Preparation (free EITC preparation)
Workforce Development and Summer/Year-Round Youth Employment Programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 500 W Loockerman St, Suite 340, Dover, DE 19904
meta_description: Delaware nonprofit providing emergency assistance, case management, housing counseling, and employment services to low-income families.
